info.title: EMHASS API
info.description: Machine-readable contract for the EMHASS JSON API. `default` values are sourced from param_definitions.json (the maintainer-declared source of truth); runtime currently loads config_defaults.json, which is kept aligned but not enforced in code.

post.summary: Convert legacy YAML config to JSON

costfun.title: Cost function
costfun.description: Define the type of cost function.

sensor_power_photovoltaics.title: Sensor power photovoltaic
sensor_power_photovoltaics.description: This is the name of the photovoltaic power-produced sensor in Watts from Home Assistant. For example: ‘sensor.power_photovoltaics’.

sensor_power_photovoltaics_forecast.title: Sensor power photovoltaic forecast
sensor_power_photovoltaics_forecast.description: This is the name of the photovoltaic forecast sensor in Watts from Home Assistant. For example: ‘sensor.p_pv_forecast’.

sensor_power_load_no_var_loads.title: Sensor power loads with no variable loads
sensor_power_load_no_var_loads.description: The name of the household power consumption sensor in Watts from Home Assistant. The deferrable loads that we will want to include in the optimization problem should be subtracted from this sensor in HASS. For example: ‘sensor.power_load_no_var_loads’

sensor_replace_zero.title: Sensor to replace NAN values with 0s
sensor_replace_zero.description: The list of retrieved variables that we would want to replace NANs (if they exist) with zeros.

sensor_linear_interp.title: Sensor to replace NAN values with linear interpolation
sensor_linear_interp.description: The list of retrieved variables that we would want to interpolate NANs values using linear interpolation

ssl_no_verify.title: SSL no verify
ssl_no_verify.description: Set to true to disable SSL verification when connecting to Home Assistant. Useful if using self-signed certificates (HTTPS with IP address).

use_websocket.title: Use Home Assistant websocket for data retrieval
use_websocket.description: Enable Home Assistant websocket as data source instead of Home Assistant API. This allows for longer historical data retention and better performance for machine learning models.

use_influxdb.title: Use InfluxDB (1.x) for data retrieval
use_influxdb.description: Enable InfluxDB as data source instead of Home Assistant API. This allows for longer historical data retention and better performance for machine learning models. Only supports version 1.x.

influxdb_host.title: InfluxDB host
influxdb_host.description: The IP address or hostname of your InfluxDB instance. Defaults to localhost.

influxdb_port.title: InfluxDB port
influxdb_port.description: The port number for your InfluxDB instance. Defaults to 8086. (unit: count)

influxdb_database.title: InfluxDB database name
influxdb_database.description: The name of the InfluxDB database containing your Home Assistant data. Defaults to 'homeassistant'.

influxdb_measurement.title: InfluxDB measurement name
influxdb_measurement.description: The measurement name where your sensor data is stored. Defaults to 'W' for the Home Assistant integration.

influxdb_retention_policy.title: InfluxDB retention policy
influxdb_retention_policy.description: The retention policy to use for InfluxDB queries. Defaults to 'autogen'.

influxdb_use_ssl.title: Use HTTPS on InfluxDB connection
influxdb_use_ssl.description: Enables HTTPS on InfluxDB connections. Defaults to 'false'.

influxdb_verify_ssl.title: Verify HTTPS on InfluxDB connection
influxdb_verify_ssl.description: Enables SSL verification of the certificate (e.g. Let's Encrypt) on InfluxDB connections. Defaults to 'false'.

continual_publish.title: Continually publish optimization results
continual_publish.description: set to True to save entities to .json after an optimization run. Then automatically republish the saved entities (with updated current state value) every freq minutes. entity data saved to data_path/entities.

logging_level.title: Logging level
logging_level.description: DEBUG provides detailed diagnostic information, INFO gives general operational messages, WARNING highlights potential issues, and ERROR indicates critical problems that may disrupt functionality.

optimization_time_step.title: Optimization steps per minute (timesteps)
optimization_time_step.description: The time step to resample retrieved data from hass. This parameter is given in minutes. It should not be defined too low or you will run into memory problems when defining the Linear Programming optimization. Defaults to 30 (unit: min)

historic_days_to_retrieve.title: Historic days to retrieve
historic_days_to_retrieve.description: We will retrieve data from now to days_to_retrieve days. Defaults to 2 (unit: days)

load_negative.title: Load negative values
load_negative.description: Set this parameter to True if the retrieved load variable is negative by convention. Defaults to False

set_zero_min.title: Remove Negatives
set_zero_min.description: Set this parameter to True to give a special treatment for a minimum value saturation to zero for power consumption data. Values below zero are replaced by nans. Defaults to True.

method_ts_round.title: Timestamp rounding method
method_ts_round.description: Set the method for timestamp rounding, options are: first, last and nearest.

delta_forecast_daily.title: Number of forecasted days
delta_forecast_daily.description: The number of days for forecasted data; can be overridden at runtime via runtimeparams. Defaults to 1. For naive-mpc, the forecast window is now extended automatically to cover a longer prediction_horizon, so this parameter usually need not be set manually for multi-day MPC runs. (unit: days)

load_forecast_method.title: Load forecast method
load_forecast_method.description: The load forecast method that will be used. The options are ‘csv’ to load a CSV file or ‘naive’ for a simple 1-day persistence model.

mlforecaster_weather_features.title: ML forecaster weather covariate columns
mlforecaster_weather_features.description: Optional list of weather covariate columns to feed the 'mlforecaster' load forecast model as exogenous inputs, in addition to the always-present calendar features. Useful when the load is weather-dependent (e.g. a heat pump). Leave empty to disable (default). Supported values: temp_air, relative_humidity, cloud_cover, wind_speed, ghi, direct_radiation, diffuse_radiation, precipitation, heating_degree, cooling_degree. The values are sourced from the Open-Meteo weather forecast; heating_degree/cooling_degree are derived from the temperature.

set_total_pv_sell.title: PV straight to grid
set_total_pv_sell.description: Set this parameter to true to consider that all the PV power produced is injected to the grid. No direct self-consumption. The default is false, for a system with direct self-consumption.

publish_deferrable_load_states.title: Publish deferrable load command states
publish_deferrable_load_states.description: When true, EMHASS additionally publishes one interpretable command sensor per deferrable load (sensor.p_deferrableN_state) whose state is 'on', 'off' or 'variable' for the current timestep, with the full optimized on/off/variable plan as a 'schedule' attribute. This lets automations act on a clear command instead of thresholding the power forecast themselves. Defaults to false.

num_threads.title: Number of threads to use for the LP solver
num_threads.description: Set the number of threads for the LP solver to use, when supported by the solver. Defaults to 0 (autodetect) (unit: count)

lp_solver_timeout.title: Linear programming solver timeout
lp_solver_timeout.description: Set the maximum time (in seconds) for the LP solver. Defaults to 45. (unit: s)

lp_solver_mip_rel_gap.title: MIP relative gap tolerance
lp_solver_mip_rel_gap.description: For mixed-integer problems (with binary variables), the solver stops when the solution is within this percentage of optimal. Valid range: 0 to 1 (0% to 100%). Higher values (e.g., 0.05 = 5%) solve faster with minimal quality loss. Recommended: 0.05 for ~2x speedup. Defaults to 0 (exact optimal). (unit: fraction)

weather_forecast_method.title: Weather forecast method
weather_forecast_method.description: This will define the weather forecast method that will be used. options are 'open-meteo', 'Solcast', 'solar.forecast' (forecast.solar) and 'csv' to load a CSV file. When loading a CSV file this will be directly considered as the PV power forecast in Watts.

weather_forecast_pv_quantile_bias.title: PV forecast conservative bias
weather_forecast_pv_quantile_bias.description: Blend factor that biases the Solcast PV forecast toward its conservative P10 (low) estimate. 0 (default) uses the central P50 estimate, i.e. unchanged behaviour. 1 uses the P10 estimate. Intermediate values blend linearly: estimate = bias*P10 + (1-bias)*P50. Higher values produce more defensive plans (more battery reserve held when solar is uncertain). Only effective with weather_forecast_method set to solcast. Defaults to 0.

open_meteo_cache_max_age.title: Open-Meteo Cache Max Age
open_meteo_cache_max_age.description: The maximum age, in minutes, of the cached open-meteo json response, after which a new version will be fetched from Open-Meteo. Defaults to 30. (unit: min)

maximum_power_from_grid.title: Max power from grid
maximum_power_from_grid.description: The maximum power that can be supplied by the utility grid in Watts (consumption). Defaults to 9000. (unit: W)

maximum_power_to_grid.title: Max export power to grid
maximum_power_to_grid.description: The maximum power that can be supplied to the utility grid in Watts (injection). Defaults to 9000. (unit: W)

inverter_is_hybrid.title: Inverter is a hybrid
inverter_is_hybrid.description: Set to True to consider that the installation inverter is hybrid for PV and batteries (Default False)

inverter_ac_output_max.title: Max hybrid inverter AC output power
inverter_ac_output_max.description: Maximum hybrid inverter output power from combined PV and battery discharge. (unit: W)

inverter_ac_input_max.title: Max hybrid inverter AC input power
inverter_ac_input_max.description: Maximum hybrid inverter input power from grid to charge battery. (unit: W)

inverter_efficiency_dc_ac.title: Hybrid inverter efficency DC to AC
inverter_efficiency_dc_ac.description: Hybrid inverter efficiency from the DC bus to AC output. (percentage/100) (unit: fraction)

inverter_efficiency_ac_dc.title: Hybrid inverter efficency AC to DC
inverter_efficiency_ac_dc.description: Hybrid inverter efficiency when charging from the AC input to DC bus. (percentage/100) (unit: fraction)

inverter_stress_cost.title: Hybrid Inverter Stress Cost
inverter_stress_cost.description: A virtual cost (Currency/kWh) applied at the inverter's nominal power to penalise high loads. Currently implemented as a quadratic curve (Cost ∝ Power²), incentivising 'low and slow' operation to reduce thermal stress and smooth power flows. (unit: ¤/kWh)

inverter_stress_segments.title: Hybrid Inverter Stress Segments
inverter_stress_segments.description: The number of linear segments used to approximate the stress cost curve. Higher values increase accuracy but may slightly increase computation time. (unit: count)

compute_curtailment.title: Set compute curtailment (grid export limit)
compute_curtailment.description: Set to True to compute a special PV curtailment variable (Default False). When enabled, curtailment that is cost-equivalent is scheduled as late as possible in the optimization horizon

load_cost_forecast_method.title: Load cost method
load_cost_forecast_method.description: Define the method that will be used for load cost forecast. The options are ‘hp_hc_periods’ for peak and non-peak hours contracts, and ‘csv’ to load custom cost from CSV file.

load_peak_hour_periods.title: List peak hour periods
load_peak_hour_periods.description: A list of peak hour periods for load consumption from the grid. This is useful if you have a contract with peak and non-peak hours.

load_peak_hours_cost.title: Peak hours electrical energy cost
load_peak_hours_cost.description: The cost of the electrical energy during peak hours (unit: ¤/kWh)

load_offpeak_hours_cost.title: Off-peak hours electrical energy cost
load_offpeak_hours_cost.description: The cost of the electrical energy during off-peak hours (unit: ¤/kWh)

production_price_forecast_method.title: PV power production price forecast method
production_price_forecast_method.description: Define the method that will be used for PV power production price forecast. This is the price that is paid by the utility for energy injected into the grid. The options are ‘constant’ for a constant fixed value or ‘csv’ to load custom price forecasts from a CSV file.

photovoltaic_production_sell_price.title: Constant PV power production price
photovoltaic_production_sell_price.description: The paid price for energy injected to the grid from excess PV production in currency/kWh. (unit: ¤/kWh)

set_use_pv.title: Enable PV system
set_use_pv.description: Set to True if we should consider an solar PV system. Defaults to False

set_use_adjusted_pv.title: Enable adjusted PV system
set_use_adjusted_pv.description: Set to True if we should consider an adjusted solar PV system. Defaults to False

adjusted_pv_regression_model.title: Regression model for adjusted PV
adjusted_pv_regression_model.description: Set the type of regression model that will be used to adjust the solar PV production to local conditions. Available models are defined in REGRESSION_METHODS (machine_learning_regressor.py). Defaults to LassoRegression

adjusted_pv_solar_elevation_threshold.title: Solar elevation threshold
adjusted_pv_solar_elevation_threshold.description: This is the solar elevation threshold parameter used to identify the early morning or late evening periods. Defaults to 10. (unit: °)

adjusted_pv_model_max_age.title: Adjusted PV model maximum age
adjusted_pv_model_max_age.description: Maximum age in hours before the adjusted PV regression model is re-fitted. If the saved model is older than this value, a new model will be trained using fresh historical data. Set to 0 to force re-fitting on every call. Defaults to 24 hours (1 day). (unit: h)

pv_module_model.title: PV module model
pv_module_model.description: The PV module model. Enter the exact CEC database name, or enter the nominal power in Watts (e.g., 300) to automatically use the closest matching panel. Can be a list for mixed orientation systems.

pv_inverter_model.title: PV inverter model
pv_inverter_model.description: The PV inverter model. Enter the exact CEC database name, or enter the nominal AC power in Watts (e.g., 5000) to automatically use the closest matching inverter. Can be a list for mixed orientation systems.

surface_tilt.title: The PV panel tilt
surface_tilt.description: The tilt angle of your solar panels. Defaults to 30. This parameter can be a list of items to enable the simulation of mixed orientation systems. (unit: °)

surface_azimuth.title: The PV azimuth (direction)
surface_azimuth.description: The azimuth of your PV installation. Defaults to 205. This parameter can be a list of items to enable the simulation of mixed orientation systems. (unit: °)

modules_per_string.title: Number of modules per string
modules_per_string.description: The number of modules per string. Defaults to 16. This parameter can be a list of items to enable the simulation of mixed orientation systems. (unit: count)

strings_per_inverter.title: Number of strings per inverter
strings_per_inverter.description: The number of used strings per inverter. Defaults to 1. This parameter can be a list of items to enable the simulation of mixed orientation systems. (unit: count)

number_of_deferrable_loads.title: Number of deferrable loads
number_of_deferrable_loads.description: Define the number of deferrable loads (appliances to shift) to consider. Defaults to 2. (unit: count)

nominal_power_of_deferrable_loads.title: Deferrable load nominal power
nominal_power_of_deferrable_loads.description: The nominal (calculated max) power for each deferrable load in Watts. (unit: W)

minimum_power_of_deferrable_loads.title: Deferrable load minimum power
minimum_power_of_deferrable_loads.description: The minimum power for each deferrable load in Watts. (unit: W)

cost_forecast_per_deferrable_load.title: Per-load cost forecast
cost_forecast_per_deferrable_load.description: Optional per-deferrable-load cost forecast track (Currency/kWh) that overrides the shared load_cost_forecast for that load. Useful for hybrid heating setups where one load (gas boiler) is priced against a gas tariff while another (heat pump) is priced against electricity. Pass null/None for any load that should keep the shared tariff. Runtime parameter - not cached.

heat_topology.title: Heat topology (graph model)
heat_topology.description: Declarative directed graph of heat sources, thermal storage, consumers, and flows. When set, EMHASS compiles this into the flat optim_conf primitives (def_load_config, shared_thermal_tanks, deferrable_load_groups, cost_forecast_per_deferrable_load) automatically. Lets you express hybrid heating setups (gas + heat pump feeding the same DHW + buffer) without hand-rolling the deferrable-load indices. See docs/heat_topology.md for the schema.

is_electric_load.title: Per-load electric-bus membership
is_electric_load.description: Per-deferrable-load boolean. True (default) means the load draws from the electric balance (heat pumps, electric heaters). False means the load consumes a non-electric commodity (gas, oil, district heating) and contributes only to its thermal target, not to the electric power balance. Auto-populated by the heat_topology compiler based on source type but can be overridden directly.

operating_hours_of_each_deferrable_load.title: Deferrable load operating hours
operating_hours_of_each_deferrable_load.description: The total number of hours that each deferrable load should operate. Fractional values are accepted (e.g. 4.5) and scheduled as the exact nominal_power * hours of energy; for control finer than the optimization timestep use operating_timesteps_of_each_deferrable_load. (unit: h)

treat_deferrable_load_as_semi_cont.title: Deferrable load as semi-continuous (on/off) variable
treat_deferrable_load_as_semi_cont.description: When configuring a deferrable load, set treat_deferrable_load_as_semi_cont = True if the device should only switch fully on or off (for example, a basic hot water heater), or set treat_deferrable_load_as_semi_cont = False if the device supports variable power control within its range (for example, an EV charger with adjustable charging rate).

set_deferrable_load_single_constant.title: Deferrable load run single constant per optimization
set_deferrable_load_single_constant.description: Define if we should set each deferrable load as a constant fixed value variable with just one startup for each optimization task

set_deferrable_startup_penalty.title: Set deferrable startup penalty
set_deferrable_startup_penalty.description: For penalty P, each time the deferrable load turns on will incur an additional cost of nominal_power (kW) * cost_of_electricity * time step at that time

deferrable_load_max_cost.title: Deferrable load max cost
deferrable_load_max_cost.description: Maximum acceptable cost for scheduling each deferrable load. If the cost to schedule the load (including startup penalty) exceeds this threshold, the load will not be scheduled. Set to 0 to disable and make load scheduling mandatory. (unit: ¤)

set_deferrable_max_startups.title: Maximum startups for deferrable loads
set_deferrable_max_startups.description: Set the maximum number of times each deferrable load can be turned on during the optimization horizon. Set to 0 to disable this limit. (unit: count)

start_timesteps_of_each_deferrable_load.title: Deferrable start timestamp
start_timesteps_of_each_deferrable_load.description: The timestep as from which each deferrable load is allowed to operate (if you don’t want the deferrable load to use the whole optimization time window). If you specify a value of 0 (or negative), the deferrable load will be optimized as from the beginning of the complete prediction horizon window. (unit: timesteps)

end_timesteps_of_each_deferrable_load.title: Deferrable end timestamp
end_timesteps_of_each_deferrable_load.description: The timestep before which each deferrable load should operate. The deferrable load is not allowed to operate after the specified time step. If a value of 0 (or negative) is provided, the deferrable load is allowed to operate in the complete optimization window) (unit: timesteps)

set_use_battery.title: Enable Battery
set_use_battery.description: Set to True if we should consider an energy storage device such as a Li-Ion battery. Defaults to False

set_nocharge_from_grid.title: Forbid charging battery from grid
set_nocharge_from_grid.description: Set this to true if you want to forbid charging the battery from the grid. The battery will only be charged from excess PV

set_nodischarge_to_grid.title: Forbid battery discharge to the grid
set_nodischarge_to_grid.description: Set this to true if you want to forbid discharging battery power to the grid.

set_battery_first_priority.title: Drain battery before grid import
set_battery_first_priority.description: Set this to true to forbid importing from the grid while the battery is still above its minimum SoC, so stored energy is used before any import. Useful on a flat (non time-of-use) tariff. Note: this can make the problem infeasible in a timestep where the load minus PV exceeds the battery maximum discharge power.

set_battery_dynamic.title: Set Battery dynamic (dis)charge power limiting
set_battery_dynamic.description: Set a power dynamic limiting condition to the battery power. This is an additional constraint on the battery dynamic in power per unit of time (timestep), which allows you to set a percentage of the battery’s nominal full power as the maximum power allowed for (dis)charge.

battery_dynamic_max.title: Maximum percentage of battery discharge per hour
battery_dynamic_max.description: The maximum positive (for discharge) allowed power variation (in percentage of battery maximum power) per hour. The solver will automatically scale this limit down based on your chosen timestep duration. (unit: fraction)

battery_dynamic_min.title: Maximum percentage of battery charge per hour
battery_dynamic_min.description: The maximum negative (for charge) allowed power variation (in percentage of battery maximum power) per hour. The solver will automatically scale this limit down based on your chosen timestep duration. (unit: fraction)

weight_battery_discharge.title: Add cost weight for battery discharge
weight_battery_discharge.description: An additional weight (currency/ kWh) applied in the cost function to battery usage for discharging (unit: ¤/kWh)

weight_battery_charge.title: Add cost weight for battery charge
weight_battery_charge.description: An additional weight (currency/ kWh) applied in the cost function to battery usage for charging (unit: ¤/kWh)

battery_discharge_power_max.title: Max battery discharge power
battery_discharge_power_max.description: The maximum discharge power in Watts (unit: W)

battery_charge_power_max.title: Max battery charge power
battery_charge_power_max.description: The maximum charge power in Watts (unit: W)

battery_nominal_energy_capacity.title: Battery total capacity
battery_nominal_energy_capacity.description: The total capacity of the battery stack in Wh (unit: Wh)

battery_minimum_state_of_charge.title: Minimum Battery charge percentage
battery_minimum_state_of_charge.description: The minimum allowable battery state of charge. (percentage/100) (unit: fraction)

battery_maximum_state_of_charge.title: Maximum Battery charge percentage
battery_maximum_state_of_charge.description: The maximum allowable battery state of charge. (percentage/100) (unit: fraction)

battery_target_state_of_charge.title: Battery desired percentage after optimization
battery_target_state_of_charge.description: The desired battery state of charge at the end of each optimization cycle. (percentage/100) (unit: fraction)

battery_stress_cost.title: Battery Stress Cost
battery_stress_cost.description: A virtual cost (Currency/kWh) applied at the battery's nominal power to penalise high loads. Currently implemented as a quadratic curve (Cost ∝ Power²), incentivising 'low and slow' operation to reduce thermal stress and smooth power flows. (unit: ¤/kWh)

battery_stress_segments.title: Battery Stress Segments
battery_stress_segments.description: The number of linear segments used to approximate the stress cost curve. Higher values increase accuracy but may slightly increase computation time. (unit: count)

battery_soc_deficit_threshold.title: Battery SOC deficit threshold
battery_soc_deficit_threshold.description: The SOC threshold below which deficit penalties apply. For example, 0.20 for 20% SOC. Penalties are applied for every hour and every kWh the SOC is below this value.

battery_soc_deficit_cost.title: Battery SOC deficit cost
battery_soc_deficit_cost.description: A virtual cost (Currency/kWh/h) applied for each kWh of SOC deficit per hour. This penalizes periods when the battery SOC falls below the deficit threshold.

battery_soc_surplus_threshold.title: Battery SOC surplus threshold
battery_soc_surplus_threshold.description: The SOC threshold above which surplus penalties apply. For example, 0.90 for 90% SOC. Penalties are applied for every hour and every kWh the SOC is above this value. Use together with battery_soc_surplus_cost to discourage long dwell near full charge.

battery_soc_surplus_cost.title: Battery SOC surplus cost
battery_soc_surplus_cost.description: A virtual cost (Currency/kWh/h) applied for each kWh of SOC above the surplus threshold per hour. This penalizes periods when the battery SOC sits above the surplus threshold, delaying charging into expected solar peaks and reducing time spent near full charge (battery longevity). Defaults to 0.0 (disabled).

capacity_cost_per_kw.title: Capacity (demand) charge cost
capacity_cost_per_kw.description: A cost (currency per kW) applied to the peak grid import power over the optimisation horizon, for tariffs with a capacity or demand charge. 0 (default) disables it and leaves the plan unchanged. A positive value makes the optimiser flatten the import peak, trading it off against energy cost in a single pass. The peak is priced once (it is a power charge, not energy), so set it to the marginal value of shaving 1 kW of peak. (unit: ¤/kW)

ignore_pv_feedback_during_curtailment.title: Ignore PV feedback during curtailment
ignore_pv_feedback_during_curtailment.description: When set to true, prevents PV forecast from being updated with real PV data, avoiding flip-flop behavior during curtailment operations

LastRun.title: EMHASS /api/v1/last-run response
LastRun.description: Response shape for GET /api/v1/last-run. AM-1's openapi generator (board item AM-1) ingests this sidecar alongside other per-endpoint schemas and emits the unified openapi.json. Authored manually until AM-1 lands.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC instant when the run completed. Null when status='no-run'.

action.description: URL-form action name (matches /action/<name>). Null when status='no-run'.

stage_times.description: Per-stage elapsed seconds, e.g. {pv_forecast: 1.2, load_forecast: 0.5}. Null when status='no-run'.

duration_total_seconds.description: Wall-clock seconds from optim-wrapper entry to record() call. Null when status='no-run'.

emhass_version.description: Installed emhass package version (importlib.metadata). Null when status='no-run'.

schema_version.description: EMHASS_SCHEMA_VERSION from command_line.py (per AC-1). Null when status='no-run'.

infeasible.description: True when the solver returned Infeasible. Null when status='no-run'.

Healthz.title: EMHASS /healthz response
Healthz.description: Liveness/readiness probe response. HTTP 200 => status 'ok'; HTTP 503 => status 'degraded'. Health is recency-only; last-run correctness is reported in last_run_status but does not affect status.

last_run_ts.description: ISO-8601 UTC (Z) timestamp of the most recent optimization run, or null if none

last_run_status.description: Raw correctness of the last run (diagnostic only; does not affect status)
